markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games’ November 2026 delay of Grand Theft Auto VI to November 19 has lengthened the timeframe for multiple high-profile events, shaping trader sentiment across the multi-outcome market. The extended window boosts implied probabilities for cultural milestones such as new albums from Rihanna or Playboi Carti and technology releases like GPT-6, while geopolitical outcomes like a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or shifts in U.S. leadership remain closely watched. Industry analysts note that Rockstar’s emphasis on polish has historically pushed back major titles, creating uncertainty that favors outcomes tied to steady cultural and tech cycles rather than sudden disruptions. Upcoming catalysts include summer 2026 music drops, fall AI announcements, and any late-year policy developments that could sway specific resolutions before the game launches.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent mistrials in Harvey Weinstein’s third New York rape retrial and ongoing appeals against his 16-year California conviction have positioned “No Prison Time” as the clear market leader at 79.6% implied probability. The 2020 New York conviction was overturned, a 2025 retrial produced a split verdict with one mistrial, and the latest June 2026 deadlock on the Jessica Mann charge leaves prosecutors weighing a potential fourth trial amid Weinstein’s health concerns at age 74. Traders appear to factor in the lengthy appeals process, possible overturns on procedural grounds, and precedent for reduced or vacated sentences in high-profile cases, while the lower-probability year-range outcomes reflect residual risk from the California term and any new convictions.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights meet in the 2026 Stanley Cup Final, with trader consensus assigning the Hurricanes a modest edge rooted in their superior regular-season record, stronger underlying metrics, and dominant playoff path that included just one loss through the first three rounds. Carolina captured the Metropolitan Division title and Eastern Conference crown while limiting opponents effectively in a 4-1 Eastern Conference Final series win over Montreal. The Golden Knights countered with a perfect Western Conference Final sweep of the Presidents' Trophy-winning Colorado Avalanche, riding timely momentum and contributions from key forwards. Home-ice advantage and recent form favor the Hurricanes slightly, yet the evenly matched series leaves realistic upset potential for either side depending on goaltending and special-teams execution.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

events.title: 2026 NBA Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NBA Finals.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold a clear edge in the 2026 NBA Finals matchup against the New York Knicks, driven by Victor Wembanyama’s All-NBA dominance and a young core assembled through high draft selections and targeted trades. The Spurs advanced past the Western Conference Finals with a Game 7 win over Oklahoma City, showcasing depth and clutch play that align with their implied probability near 64 percent. New York counters with strong momentum from an 11-game playoff winning streak, including a sweep of the Eastern Conference Finals, fueled by Jalen Brunson’s leadership and Karl-Anthony Towns’ interior presence after roster moves via free agency and trades. Home-court advantage in the series further supports the current trader consensus reflected in these probabilities.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: European squads lead trader consensus in the expanded 48-team field, with France and Spain narrowly ahead on the back of strong recent form and deep rosters heading into the June 11 kickoff. Spain’s European Championship pedigree and attacking talent, including recovery updates around key attackers, underpin its position, while France’s blend of experience and depth sustains its implied probability near the top. England benefits from consistent qualification and squad stability, keeping it competitive with Argentina’s defending-champion core and Brazil’s traditional strength. The bunched pricing among the leading contenders reflects comparable paths through the draw, recent continental results, and the absence of decisive pre-tournament shifts that would separate the field.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping's entrenched control over the Chinese Communist Party, its central institutions, and the People's Liberation Army underpins the strong trader consensus that he will remain in power through 2026. No term limits constrain his continued leadership, and the absence of an anointed successor or visible factional challenges ahead of the 2027 Party Congress has reinforced expectations of a fourth term extending well beyond that date. Recent public appearances, including high-level diplomacy in mid-2026, have further signaled continuity rather than transition. While historical precedents and occasional unverified rumors have prompted short-term speculation, verifiable developments show sustained authority without the procedural or political triggers that would alter this positioning.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination cycle still years from its first primaries and caucuses, traders reflect a wide-open field where Gavin Newsom holds the largest share due to his executive record as California governor and sustained national visibility. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws support from progressive constituencies, while Kamala Harris benefits from prior vice-presidential and nominee experience. Key differentiators include state-level records, ideological positioning, and donor networks. Consolidation behind one outcome could occur through shifts in early polling averages, midterm results, endorsements from party leaders, or emerging frontrunner dynamics in the coming cycle.
